solution: 
```java
class MinStack {
    private Stack<int[]> stack;

    public MinStack() {
        stack = new Stack<>();
    }
    
    public void push(int val) {
        if (stack.isEmpty()) {
            stack.push(new int[]{val, val});
        } else {
            int currentMin = Math.min(val, stack.peek()[1]);
            stack.push(new int[]{val, currentMin});
        }
    }
    
    public void pop() {
        stack.pop();
    }
    
    public int top() {
        return stack.peek()[0];
    }
    
    public int getMin() {
        return stack.peek()[1];
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution uses a stack of integer arrays where each element stores the current value and the minimum value up to that point. This allows all operations to be performed in constant time. The main strength is the efficient time complexity for all operations, while the weakness is the increased space usage due to storing the minimum value with each element.>
